channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Your AI Coding Assistant Has No Memory of Why
item.description: A pull request lands. Two approvals, tests green, CI blue. It ships on a Tuesday. Three weeks later there's an incident. Somebody opens the file and finds a branch nobody remembers discussing. It...

item.title: 9 decisions I make before I open Cursor
item.description: I've shipped a lot of side projects. Most of them went nowhere. Not because the code was bad. The code was fine. Cursor and Claude Code made the code the easy part. They went nowhere because I...

item.title: Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access
item.description: Threat actors have been observed exploiting a now-patched high-severity Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS vulnerability as an entry point to deploy Qilin (aka Agenda) ransomware on victim environments....

item.title: Kafka Topics, Partitions, and Offsets Explained: The Concepts Every Beginner Confuses
item.description: When I first started learning Apache Kafka, I quickly realized that three terms appeared everywhere: topics, partitions, and offsets. At first glance, they seemed straightforward, but...

item.title: The Meter Is Always Running
item.description: The Meter Is Always Running For forty years, enterprise software rested on a comfortable assumption: that it was a fixed cost. You paid a license, amortized it over a depreciation schedule, and...

item.title: How I Fixed Hallucinations by Switching from AGENTS.md to AGENTS.db
item.description: Before I dive in, a bit of background on how our agentic workflow at Exam Intelligence for deep analysis and notes generation works... Initially, I tried to create a complete end-to-end LangGraph...

item.title: Astro Server Islands: Keep Pages Fast Without Giving Up Interactivity
item.description: Most content sites do not need a full SPA. They need fast HTML first, then a small amount of JavaScript for carousels, comments, or filters. That is exactly what Astro Server Islands are for....

item.title: The table looked general-purpose. The schema disagreed.
item.description: A CHECK constraint is documentation with teeth. That's the whole lesson. But here's what it looks like when you learn it at runtime instead of at read-time. Key Takeaways A CHECK constraint...

item.title: Rails already ships class_names. I built clsx anyway.
item.description: When I published a little gem for conditional CSS classes two years ago, the first comment was Adrien Poly asking the obvious thing: "What is the main difference with rails class_names helper?" My...

item.title: When to Break and When to Bend: Post-Quantum Migration Tradeoffs in Rust
item.description: Bonus post in the Anyhide series. The original 6-post arc closed in July; this post is about a meaty migration that landed in v0.14 — moving the whole stack to post-quantum hybrid encryption. Most...

item.title: XChaCha20-Poly1305 in a Mesh Network: A Practical Deep Dive
item.description: Most encryption tutorials stop at "use AES-256-GCM." But in a mesh network, the threat model is different. Keys rotate constantly. Messages arrive out of order. Some nodes are compromised. You...

item.title: A touchscreen and light make the new X4 Pro the best version of Xteink’s tiny e-readers
item.description: The familiar story with Xteink’s tiny e-readers plays out once again with its new X4 Pro: upgraded hardware held back by frustrating software. The company’s latest tiny e-reader adds a touchscreen...

item.title: Garmin’s new screen-free fitness tracker doesn’t require a subscription
item.description: Garmin announced a new smart band today designed to track "advanced fitness and wellness features" without a screen to eliminate distractions and push its battery life to up to 10 days between...

item.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
item.description: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June

item.title: The Switch 2 is $50 off at Woot for new customers
item.description: Woot is celebrating its 22nd anniversary by rolling out a full week of sales, and one of them stands out for gamers looking to get a deal on Nintendo’s latest console. Normally $449.99, the...

item.title: Zimbra Patches Critical SNMP Command Injection and Four XSS Vulnerabilities
item.description: Zimbra has rolled out fixes to address multiple critical security issues, including a command injection flaw in the Simple Network Management Protocol (SNMP) monitoring component. As many as nine...

item.title: Halliday’s New Smart Glasses Skip the Camera
item.description: Halliday’s G2 glasses can listen to and summarize your workplace meetings—with no video involved.

item.title: The wireless headset that gets hot-swappable batteries right
item.description: New gaming gear is always promising to shave milliseconds off latency or response time. The improvements usually aren't obvious. But the Glorious GHS InfinitePlay wireless headset, on the other...

item.title: Nintendo keeps making shooters more friendly and approachable
item.description: Nintendo does a lot of weird things, but Splatoon might just be one of the strangest - or at least the boldest. The series started as an attempt to muscle into the ultra-competitive online shooter...

item.title: Halliday’s latest smart glasses feature a much-improved display
item.description: I first slipped on Halliday's original smart glasses at CES 2025. I was not a fan. The glasses had a tiny, movable display window embedded into the frame that was incredibly finicky to see, and my...

item.title: The Download: Chinese AI divides the White House, and a record copyright payout
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself...

item.title: Open-Source Android AI Agents Could Let Invisible Screen Text Run Code on Host PCs
item.description: An Android app that can draw over other windows and write to shared storage can slip instructions to the AI agent driving that phone, in text no human eye will ever see. Two more steps, and the...

item.title: N-day is Becoming N-Hour. Patching Faster Won't Save You.
item.description: Every patch is a confession. The moment a vendor ships a security fix, the diff between the old code and the new code tells anyone watching exactly what was broken and where. Turn that diff back...

item.title: New Bit2Watt Attack Could Let Cloud Tenants Disrupt Power Grids Without an Exploit
item.description: A cloud tenant using nothing but ordinary GPU access can push a data center's power draw up and down fast enough to threaten the grid it runs on, with no exploit and no break-in. That is the claim...

item.title: America needs to stop getting shocked by Chinese AI
item.description: Last week, two Chinese AI companies unveiled models they say can credibly compete with the best systems from OpenAI and Anthropic. The response was swift and predictable. Markets wobbled,...

item.title: MIT to Become Hotbed of AI Video Surveillance
item.description: It’s a lot: According to information obtained by The Tech, MIT is spending over $3 million on more than 500 AI surveillance cameras in academic buildings, residence halls, and outdoor areas along...

item.title: Advancing next-gen AI with materials science innovation
item.description: The conversation about AI often centers on algorithms, computing power, or huge investments in new semiconductor fabrication plants and hyperscale data centers. But beneath each of these advances...

item.title: ‘It’s a Modern-Day Draft’: Why Stanford Students Walked Out on Sundar Pichai’s Commencement Speech
item.description: Last month, more than a hundred Stanford students left their own graduation to protest Google’s military contracts and deals with ICE. Two organizers, Amanda Campos and Eva Jones, tell us why.

item.title: Who’s afraid of the big, bad GPU?
item.description: How does AI make you feel? Are you excited to “vibe-code” your smart home? Or anxious about all the added pollution and billions of gallons of water used by data centers? Dig a little deeper and...

item.title: Gritt exits stealth with $32 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
item.description: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plans to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.
